Psychic: The soul only suppresses its desires, the forced denial breeding the frustration it meant to escape.

attributes.attribute: Belief I AM What Is Sought
attributes.title: Belief I AM What Is Sought (Mukta Abhimana)
attributes.aspect: ·   I.11 - 'Mukta-abhimani mukto hi, baddho baddhabhimanyapi' The nature of this aspect. Ashtavakra's second 'explosive Moksha-capsule': Mukta-abhimani mukto—he who considers himself liberated IS liberated; he who considers himself bound IS bound. This is not positive thinking but recognition: the only bondage is the conviction of bondage. Convince yourself of freedom and you are free.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: holding ‘I am what is sought’ (mukta-abhimāna) — identifying with the goal.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: holding ‘I am what is sought’ (mukta-abhimāna) — identifying with the goal

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep conviction—not affirmation—that one is already free. The recognition transforms experience.
facets.somatic: Body lived from conviction of freedom.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as expression of innate freedom.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in 'I am free' as recognition, not assertion.
facets.psychic: The soul takes the stand that it already is what it seeks, the conviction itself loosening the search.

balanced.text:
Deep conviction—not affirmation—that one is already free. The recognition transforms experience.

Somatic: Body lived from conviction of freedom.

Emotional: Emotional life as expression of innate freedom.

Cognitive: Mind anchored in 'I am free' as recognition, not assertion.

Psychic: The soul takes the stand that it already is what it seeks, the conviction itself loosening the search.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Affirmations chanted ('I am free, I am free') without inner conviction.
facets.somatic: Body unchanged by verbal claims.
facets.emotional: Emotional reactivity contradicting affirmations.
facets.cognitive: Mental repetition without genuine conviction.
facets.psychic: The ego appropriates the stand, 'I am the free one' becoming spiritual inflation.

excess.text:
Affirmations chanted ('I am free, I am free') without inner conviction.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Deep conviction of bondage. 'I am limited, I am suffering' as core identity.
facets.somatic: Body lived from conviction of bondage.
facets.emotional: Emotional life confirms bondage.
facets.cognitive: Mind anchored in 'I am bound.'
facets.psychic: The soul is convinced it is only a bound, seeking creature, the sought-for forever elsewhere.

deficient.text:
Deep conviction of bondage. 'I am limited, I am suffering' as core identity.

attributes.attribute: Mind Control as By-Product of Self-Knowledge
attributes.title: Mind Control as By-Product of Self-Knowledge (Not Practice)
attributes.aspect: ·   XVIII.51 The nature of this aspect. Ashtavakra's distinct teaching contradicting Patanjali: don't try to control the mind through practice (chitta-vritti-nirodha). Through direct recognition of the Self, mind-control becomes automatic. Brahma the creator says: 'No untruth comes from my mouth, my mind never wanders—not because I cultivated these qualities but because I hold Hari in my heart.'

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: mind-control as the by-product of Self-knowledge, not a separate practice.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: mind-control as the by-product of Self-knowledge, not a separate practice

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Self-knowledge naturally producing mental stillness. No fight with mind; only recognition.
facets.somatic: Body becomes calm as Self is recognized.
facets.emotional: Emotional stillness as natural consequence.
facets.cognitive: Mind becomes still without forcing—because the witnesser-witnessed structure dissolves.
facets.psychic: The soul sees that the mind quiets of itself as self-knowledge dawns, control a fruit rather than a forced aim.

balanced.text:
Self-knowledge naturally producing mental stillness. No fight with mind; only recognition.

Somatic: Body becomes calm as Self is recognized.

Emotional: Emotional stillness as natural consequence.

Cognitive: Mind becomes still without forcing—because the witnesser-witnessed structure dissolves.

Psychic: The soul sees that the mind quiets of itself as self-knowledge dawns, control a fruit rather than a forced aim.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Claim of mind-control without genuine recognition. Forced suppression masquerading as wisdom.
facets.somatic: Body tense from suppression.
facets.emotional: Emotional pressure from forcing stillness.
facets.cognitive: Mind in war with itself.
facets.psychic: The soul wages war on the mind directly, the very effort to control it stirring it further.

excess.text:
Claim of mind-control without genuine recognition. Forced suppression masquerading as wisdom.

Somatic: Body tense from suppression.

Cognitive: Mind in war with itself.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Endless mind-control practice without Self-recognition. Or no practice and no recognition.
facets.somatic: Body in repetitive practice without breakthrough.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns unchanged despite practice.
facets.cognitive: Mind being whipped through exercises with no transformation.
facets.psychic: The soul neither knows the Self nor stills the mind, its thoughts running unchecked.

deficient.text:
Endless mind-control practice without Self-recognition. Or no practice and no recognition.

attributes.title: Choiceless Perception (Beyond Good-Bad Judging)
attributes.aspect: ·   Throughout text The nature of this aspect. Ashtavakra's distinctive teaching: perception itself can be choiceless. Not non-perception, but perception without judgment-overlay. Events happen; the witness sees them without categorizing as good/bad. This is not moral relativism but transcendence of dualistic categorization. The witnesser perceives what is without imposing preferences.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: State — an attained condition · Valence: The fruit · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual, transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: perception beyond good-bad judging — seeing without the verdict.
coordinate.key_relation: perception beyond good-bad judging — seeing without the verdict

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Perception without immediate good/bad overlay. Events recognized in their actual nature.
facets.somatic: Body's perceptions clear of judgment-overlay.
facets.emotional: Emotional perceptions without immediate preference.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees without categorizing.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives without the constant overlay of good-bad judgment, seeing what is before labeling it.

balanced.text:
Perception without immediate good/bad overlay. Events recognized in their actual nature.

Emotional: Emotional perceptions without immediate preference.

Cognitive: Mind sees without categorizing.

Psychic: The soul perceives without the constant overlay of good-bad judgment, seeing what is before labeling it.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced choicelessness that becomes moral indifference. Refusing necessary discrimination.
facets.somatic: Body accepting harm without protection.
facets.emotional: Emotional dissociation called witnessing.
facets.cognitive: Mental refusal to make even necessary distinctions.
facets.psychic: The soul uses 'beyond judgment' to dull its discernment, refusing to see real harm.

excess.text:
Forced choicelessness that becomes moral indifference. Refusing necessary discrimination.

Somatic: Body accepting harm without protection.

Cognitive: Mental refusal to make even necessary distinctions.

Psychic: The soul uses 'beyond judgment' to dull its discernment, refusing to see real harm.

facets.overview: Every perception immediately categorized as good/bad/pleasant/unpleasant. Continuous judgment.
facets.somatic: Body in constant judgment-driven reactions.
facets.emotional: Emotional turbulence from constant evaluation.
facets.cognitive: Mind in perpetual binary-judging.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives only through judgment, every sight instantly sorted into good and bad.

deficient.text:
Every perception immediately categorized as good/bad/pleasant/unpleasant. Continuous judgment.

attributes.title: Forget Everything (Sarva Smriti Tyaga)
attributes.aspect: ·   Byrom's chapter title: 'Forget Everything' The nature of this aspect. Ashtavakra's instruction to forget everything: not amnesia but release of accumulated mental content as defining identity. Memories make 'me'; concepts make 'understanding'; scriptures make 'knower.' All these can be released. What remains when everything is forgotten? Pure awareness, which is one's nature. Forgetting is not loss but liberation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: letting go of all memory (sarva-smṛti-tyāga) into the present.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: letting go of all memory (sarva-smṛti-tyāga) into the present

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Active releasing of mental content as identity-foundation. Memory remains functional but doesn't define self.
facets.somatic: Body lives without burden of cumulative history.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns released as identity-defining.
facets.cognitive: Mind operates without accumulated content as 'me.'
facets.psychic: The soul learns to set down its whole accumulated story, resting in the freshness before memory.

balanced.text:
Active releasing of mental content as identity-foundation. Memory remains functional but doesn't define self.

Somatic: Body lives without burden of cumulative history.

Emotional: Emotional patterns released as identity-defining.

Cognitive: Mind operates without accumulated content as 'me.'

Psychic: The soul learns to set down its whole accumulated story, resting in the freshness before memory.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced amnesia—trying to suppress memory rather than release identification with it.
facets.somatic: Body damaged through dissociation.
facets.emotional: Emotional repression rather than release.
facets.cognitive: Mental dissociation called transcendence.
facets.psychic: The soul mistakes mere blankness or avoidance for the sacred forgetting.

excess.text:
Forced amnesia—trying to suppress memory rather than release identification with it.

Somatic: Body damaged through dissociation.

Emotional: Emotional repression rather than release.

Cognitive: Mental dissociation called transcendence.

Psychic: The soul mistakes mere blankness or avoidance for the sacred forgetting.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Total identification with accumulated content. Memory IS me; concepts ARE me.
facets.somatic: Body identified through accumulated history.
facets.emotional: Emotional patterns from past ARE self.
facets.cognitive: Mind crowded with content all claimed as 'me.'
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly bound by its remembered story, unable to set anything down.

deficient.text:
Total identification with accumulated content. Memory IS me; concepts ARE me.

Emotional: Emotional patterns from past ARE self.

attributes.attribute: Look Deeply at the World and Dispose of It
attributes.title: Look Deeply at the World and Dispose of It
attributes.aspect: ·   Atmananda's Introduction The nature of this aspect. Atmananda's profound technique: don't flee from world or cultivate forced dispassion. Look at the world deeply enough that you SEE through it. Look at passion deeply enough that it dissolves on its own. The ego itself contains the Real—it is only expanded mind. Morality is consequence of realization, not prerequisite.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna) · Valence: Cultivated method · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: looking deeply at the world and setting it aside — seeing through to dispose of.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Practice / method — self-inquiry (the path of Jñāna)
coordinate.key_relation: looking deeply at the world and setting it aside — seeing through to dispose of

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Deep penetrating examination of every experience, every passion, every object. Through seeing, the spell is broken.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in deep examination, not avoidance.
facets.emotional: Emotions analyzed at their root, not suppressed.
facets.cognitive: Mind looks into every aspect of experience until it sees through.
facets.psychic: The soul looks so deeply into the world that it sees through it, and so can hold it lightly and set it down.

balanced.text:
Deep penetrating examination of every experience, every passion, every object. Through seeing, the spell is broken.

Somatic: Body engaged in deep examination, not avoidance.

Emotional: Emotions analyzed at their root, not suppressed.

Cognitive: Mind looks into every aspect of experience until it sees through.

Psychic: The soul looks so deeply into the world that it sees through it, and so can hold it lightly and set it down.

excess.label: Excessive analysis becoming intellectual neurosis. Endless dissection without resolution.

facets.somatic: Body in obsessive examination-mode.
facets.emotional: Emotional life as constant case-study.
facets.cognitive: Mind dissecting endlessly without integration.
facets.psychic: The soul's 'seeing through' curdles into contempt, a premature disposal of a life still to be lived.

excess.text:
Somatic: Body in obsessive examination-mode.

Emotional: Emotional life as constant case-study.

Cognitive: Mind dissecting endlessly without integration.

Psychic: The soul's 'seeing through' curdles into contempt, a premature disposal of a life still to be lived.

facets.overview: Avoiding world and passion through repression or distraction. Never looking deeply enough.
facets.somatic: Body in avoidance patterns.
facets.emotional: Emotional suppression rather than analysis.
facets.cognitive: Mind flees from genuine examination.
facets.psychic: The soul never looks deeply, taking the world's surface as final and clinging to it whole.

deficient.text:
Avoiding world and passion through repression or distraction. Never looking deeply enough.

Somatic: Body in avoidance patterns.

Emotional: Emotional suppression rather than analysis.

Cognitive: Mind flees from genuine examination.

Psychic: The soul never looks deeply, taking the world's surface as final and clinging to it whole.

attributes.title: Mauna (Inner Silence)
attributes.aspect: मौन   ·   Chapter 17 The nature of this aspect. Mauna is not merely external silence but the inner stillness of mind. It is the absence of mental chatter, the deep quietude in which true wisdom and Self-realization can emerge. It is a form of mental austerity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: inner silence (mauna) — the stilling of speech and mind.
coordinate.key_relation: inner silence (mauna) — the stilling of speech and mind

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Mauna is practiced wisely, a person experiences both external restraint of speech and inner stillness of mind. There is appropriate use of silence as well as speech, with deep inner quietude.
facets.somatic: Calm nervous system, healthy body from reduced stress, physical ease from inner peace, balanced respiratory function from quietude.
facets.emotional: Emotional peace from inner silence, freedom from emotional chatter, depth of feeling from stillness, emotional healing through quietude.
facets.cognitive: Mental stillness and peace, freedom from inner chatter, deep wisdom emerging from silence, clarity from quietude, contemplative depth.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps an inner silence beneath its words, the still center from which speech and rest both arise.

balanced.text:
When Mauna is practiced wisely, a person experiences both external restraint of speech and inner stillness of mind. There is appropriate use of silence as well as speech, with deep inner quietude.

Somatic: Calm nervous system, healthy body from reduced stress, physical ease from inner peace, balanced respiratory function from quietude.

Emotional: Emotional peace from inner silence, freedom from emotional chatter, depth of feeling from stillness, emotional healing through quietude.

Cognitive: Mental stillness and peace, freedom from inner chatter, deep wisdom emerging from silence, clarity from quietude, contemplative depth.

Psychic: The soul keeps an inner silence beneath its words, the still center from which speech and rest both arise.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Excessive Mauna without wisdom becomes withdrawal from necessary communication, isolation, inability to engage with others, and avoidance disguised as spiritual practice.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal from interaction, isolation that damages health, social disengagement affecting well-being.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal and isolation, inability to connect through expression, lonely emotional state, avoidance of genuine engagement.
facets.cognitive: Mental withdrawal from world, inability to communicate when needed, false spiritual pride about silence, avoidance of necessary engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul makes silence a rigid rule or a display, the outer quiet masking an inner noise.

attributes.title: Tapas (Austerity)
attributes.aspect: तपस्   ·   Chapter 17 The nature of this aspect. Tapas is austerity and disciplined effort that transforms consciousness. It exists in three forms: physical (worship, cleanliness, non-violence), verbal (truthful pleasant speech, scripture study), and mental (serenity, silence, self-control). It is the fire of transformation.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Tapas is balanced and sattvic, a person practices appropriate physical, verbal, and mental discipline that purifies and transforms. There is healthy intensity without harm.
facets.somatic: Physical discipline that builds health, body strengthened through appropriate austerity, vitality from purifying practices, balanced physical refinement.
facets.emotional: Emotional purification through discipline, emotional strength from practice, healing through sustained effort, transformed emotional patterns.
facets.cognitive: Mental purification through discipline, clarity from practice, refined thinking, transformation of mental patterns through sustained effort.
facets.psychic: The soul takes up wholesome austerity, the disciplined heat that burns away impurity and tempers the will.

attributes.title: Sravanam (Listening to Sacred Stories)
attributes.aspect: ·   7.5.23 - First of nine bhakti processes The nature of this aspect. Sravanam is the first of the nine devotional practices: actively listening to sacred stories, names, and pastimes of the Divine. In daily life, this means consciously choosing what one listens to — replacing gossip, complaint, and trivial talk with stories that elevate. The Bhagavatam itself is meant to be heard, not just read. Modern equivalent: podcasts, satsangs, recorded teachings consumed daily. Sravanam means listening — to scriptural stories, divine names, accounts of devotees. In daily life: listening replaces gossip; the type of input the ears receive shapes consciousness. 'Bhakti washes away all karma. Must be listened for 7 days' (the traditional Saptaha format).

facets.cognitive: Mind continuously fed sacred content; gossip and triviality lose their hold.
facets.psychic: The soul nourishes itself on sacred story, the hearing of the Divine's deeds turning the heart toward it.

attributes.title: Smaranam (Remembering the Divine)
attributes.aspect: ·   7.5.23 - Third of nine bhakti processes The nature of this aspect. Smaranam is continuous remembrance — keeping the Divine in mind as a background thread throughout daily activities. Not formal meditation but the practice of returning attention to the Sacred at every available moment: while waiting, walking, between tasks, during sleep onset. The thread holds even when activities require attention elsewhere. Smaranam is continuous remembrance throughout the day. Not formal meditation but the ongoing background-thread of remembering the Sacred. While working, eating, conversing — the thread continues. Like Brahma whose mind 'never wandered because he held Hari in his heart.'

attributes.title: Dasyam (Servant-Attitude)
attributes.aspect: ·   7.5.23 - Seventh of nine bhakti processes The nature of this aspect. Dasyam is the cultivated attitude of being the Divine's servant — viewing oneself as nitya-krishna-dasa (eternally a servant of Krishna). In daily life this transforms work, responsibilities, and duties. The arrogance of 'I am the doer' dissolves; service becomes a privilege rather than burden. Cures both inflated self-importance and chronic resentment of duties. Dasyam is the attitude of being servant of the Divine. In daily life this transforms how one approaches work, family duties, responsibilities. 'I am eternally a servant of Krishna' (nitya-krishna-dasa). Every duty becomes sacred service. Cures arrogance and entitlement.

attributes.title: Sakhyam (Friendship with the Divine)
attributes.aspect: ·   7.5.23 - Eighth of nine bhakti processes The nature of this aspect. Sakhyam is cultivating friendship with the Divine — a relationship of intimate companionship rather than distant worship. Krishna is described as suhridam sarva-bhutanam, the great well-wishing friend of all beings. In daily life: confiding in the Divine as friend, joking and playing in mind, the sense of constant companionship. Profound cure for existential loneliness. Sakhyam is friendship with the Divine — like Arjuna with Krishna, intimate companionship. The Divine becomes 'the great well-wishing friend' (suhridam sarva-bhutanam). One is never alone. Cures existential loneliness.

attributes.title: Trusteeship (Use What You Need, Hold Rest in Trust)
attributes.aspect: ·   Canto 1, Sutra Goswami's teaching The nature of this aspect. Suta Goswami's foundational instruction for householders: live as trustee, not owner. Use what is necessary for legitimate maintenance; the rest is held in trust for the Divine and for those in need. This is not poverty or asceticism but conscious stewardship. Reverses ordinary consumer mentality without forcing renunciation. Suta Goswami's daily-life instruction: 'Do all action for the pleasure of the Lord. Whatever is necessary for maintenance, use that much; the rest hold as trustee.' Revolutionary economics — one is not owner but steward.

attributes.attribute: Aspire to Long Life for Sacred Hearing
attributes.title: Aspire to Long Life for Sacred Hearing
attributes.aspect: ·   Canto 1 The nature of this aspect. Suta Goswami's reframing of life-purpose: 'Don't live life for desire-fulfillment; live to attain satchitananda state.' The Bhagavatam tells stories of the Lord's 24 incarnations; one needs adequate time to hear, contemplate, integrate all this. Long life is desired not for more pleasure but for more spiritual unfolding. 'Aspire to live a long life, to listen to the whole story of Bhagavan. Bhagavan took 24 lives.' Reframes life-purpose: not desire-fulfillment but ample time for sacred listening, learning, devotional development.

attributes.title: Reading Bhagavata in Saptaha (7-Day Format)
attributes.aspect: ·   Class notes - 'Must be listened for 7 days' The nature of this aspect. The traditional Bhagavata Saptaha is a 7-day continuous narration. In modern daily life, this principle adapts: schedule periods of immersion in sacred study — annual 7-day retreat; weekly multi-hour study; daily 30 minutes. The cumulative effect transforms life. Even non-Hindus benefit from immersive engagement with this remarkable text. The Bhagavatam is traditionally heard in 7-day intensive (saptaha) format. In daily life: schedule intensive immersion periods — annual retreat, weekly study, daily reading. Even just 30 minutes daily of reading Bhagavatam transforms life over months.

facets.overview: No regular sacred study. Spiritual life without textual nourishment. Disconnect from tradition.
facets.somatic: Body never engaged in sustained study.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without scriptural input.
facets.cognitive: Mind without sacred reading.
facets.psychic: The soul never immerses itself in sacred reading, the scriptures unopened and untasted.

attributes.title: Attunement With Reality (Yoga as Resonance)
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 1 The nature of this aspect. Attunement with Reality is the foundational orientation of all spiritual practice. Yoga is not seeking another world but tuning to the actual nature of THIS world correctly. Most suffering comes from being out-of-tune with reality—either denying it or imagining it differently than it is.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: yoga as resonance — attunement with reality.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: yoga as resonance — attunement with reality

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living in continuous attunement to actual reality—what is, not what should be. Acceptance combined with engagement.
facets.somatic: Body in tune with natural rhythms, biological needs honored, no fighting with body.
facets.emotional: Emotional life in tune with what actually is, not denying or projecting.
facets.cognitive: Mind aligned with reality, no constant fight against what is.
facets.psychic: The soul attunes itself to the real, yoga as a resonance in which the inner string vibrates with the cosmic.

balanced.text:
Living in continuous attunement to actual reality—what is, not what should be. Acceptance combined with engagement.

Somatic: Body in tune with natural rhythms, biological needs honored, no fighting with body.

Emotional: Emotional life in tune with what actually is, not denying or projecting.

Cognitive: Mind aligned with reality, no constant fight against what is.

Psychic: The soul attunes itself to the real, yoga as a resonance in which the inner string vibrates with the cosmic.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Fatalistic 'acceptance' of harmful conditions as 'reality'—mistaking passive resignation for attunement.
facets.somatic: Body neglected as 'this is just reality.'
facets.emotional: Emotional surrender to unacceptable situations.
facets.cognitive: Mental abdication of agency under guise of attunement.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a felt 'attunement,' chasing peak resonant states rather than living in tune.

Psychic: The soul forces a felt 'attunement,' chasing peak resonant states rather than living in tune.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Constant fight with reality—wanting things to be different, imagining alternate worlds. Maximum suffering.
facets.somatic: Body in chronic stress from fighting natural rhythms.
facets.emotional: Emotional turmoil from rejecting what is.
facets.cognitive: Mental civil war against reality, no peace possible.
facets.psychic: The soul lives wholly out of tune, jarring against reality at every turn.

attributes.title: Right Relationships Between Things
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 2 The nature of this aspect. All of existence is relational. There are no isolated entities; everything stands in relation to everything else. Wrong relationships (clinging, aversion, control, exploitation) create suffering. Right relationships (reverence, appropriateness, balance) create harmony.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: seeing the right relationships between things — harmony as knowledge.
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized
coordinate.key_relation: seeing the right relationships between things — harmony as knowledge

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: All relationships—with self, body, others, things, cosmos—established appropriately. Harmony as natural result.
facets.somatic: Right relationship with body—neither indulgent nor harsh.
facets.emotional: Right relationships with others—neither codependent nor isolated.
facets.cognitive: Mind in right relationship with thoughts—neither suppressing nor identified.
facets.psychic: The soul perceives the right relationship of each thing to each, and so moves through life in harmony with the whole.

balanced.text:

Somatic: Right relationship with body—neither indulgent nor harsh.

Emotional: Right relationships with others—neither codependent nor isolated.

Cognitive: Mind in right relationship with thoughts—neither suppressing nor identified.

Psychic: The soul perceives the right relationship of each thing to each, and so moves through life in harmony with the whole.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid 'correct relationship' rules—formulaic ethics without genuine attunement.
facets.somatic: Body in mechanical 'proper' postures.
facets.emotional: Emotional relationships forced into formulas.
facets.cognitive: Mental rules without genuine wisdom.
facets.psychic: The soul over-systematizes relationships into rigid schemes, the living web reduced to a diagram.

facets.somatic: Body abused through wrong relationship.
facets.emotional: Toxic emotional relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mind in wrong relationship with itself and others.
facets.psychic: The soul sees only disconnected things, blind to the relationships that bind them into a whole.

attributes.title: Yoga as Balance (Samatvam Yoga Uchyate)
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 4 (echoing Bhagavad Gita 2.48) The nature of this aspect. Yoga, in its essential meaning, is balance—not just physical postures but the equilibrium of one's whole being. Body, breath, mind, emotion, spirit all in dynamic balance. When any dimension dominates, yoga is broken.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: ‘yoga is equanimity’ (samatvam, Gītā 2.48); kin to Samatvam (Dim 29).

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Dynamic equilibrium across all dimensions—body, breath, mind, emotion, spirit in harmonious balance.
facets.somatic: Body balanced in posture, function, rest and activity.
facets.emotional: Emotional balance—neither suppression nor indulgence.
facets.cognitive: Mental equilibrium across all engagements.
facets.psychic: The soul lives the Gītā's definition of yoga as evenness, equal-minded in success and failure alike.

balanced.text:

Psychic: The soul lives the Gītā's definition of yoga as evenness, equal-minded in success and failure alike.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Rigid pursuit of perfect 'balance' becoming itself imbalance—obsessive equalization.
facets.somatic: Body forced into 'balance' that ignores natural variations.
facets.emotional: Emotional flatlining mistaken for balance.
facets.cognitive: Mental obsession with maintaining balance.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a flat composure, mistaking numbness for the living evenness of the sage.

facets.somatic: Body imbalanced—either over-exercise or sedentary, over-eat or starve.
facets.emotional: Emotional extremes without modulation.
facets.cognitive: Mental fixation on one engagement, neglect of others.
facets.psychic: The soul is tossed by every outcome, its inner balance hostage to results.

attributes.title: Possessing Nothing (Aparigraha as Liberation)
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 8 The nature of this aspect. We imagine we possess things—body, mind, relationships, objects, knowledge. But examination reveals we possess NOTHING; all is temporary use. Recognizing this releases the enormous burden of pseudo-ownership and opens lived experience to spontaneous freedom.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: non-possession as liberation (aparigraha); same as the yama (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: non-possession as liberation (aparigraha); same as the yama (Dim 11)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Living with the recognition of non-possession. Using everything fully without claiming ownership. Profound lightness.
facets.somatic: Body recognized as temporary loan, used well without grasping.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from possessiveness in relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mind free from possession-claims about ideas, knowledge.
facets.psychic: The soul holds nothing as its own, and in possessing nothing finds itself unburdened and free.

balanced.text:

Somatic: Body recognized as temporary loan, used well without grasping.

Psychic: The soul holds nothing as its own, and in possessing nothing finds itself unburdened and free.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced non-possession becoming pathological poverty, refusing legitimate use of things.
facets.somatic: Body deprived of legitimate needs in name of aparigraha.
facets.emotional: Emotional rejection of healthy attachments.
facets.cognitive: Mental rigidity around possession-rejection.
facets.psychic: The soul makes non-possession a performance of poverty, clinging to its very renunciation.

facets.somatic: Body experienced as 'my body' with intense ownership.
facets.emotional: Emotional possessiveness in all relationships.
facets.cognitive: Mental ownership of ideas, opinions, identities.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps and hoards, weighed down by possessions it mistakes for security.

attributes.title: Renunciation (Sannyasa as Inward Movement)
attributes.sanskrit: संन्यास   ·   Chapter 20 - 'Neither by work nor by progeny nor by wealth, but by renunciation alone immortality is attained.'
attributes.aspect: संन्यास   ·   Chapter 20 - 'Neither by work nor by progeny nor by wealth, but by renunciation alone immortality is attained.' The nature of this aspect. Sannyasa is not external withdrawal but inner cessation of outward movement of consciousness. Every desire is movement outward; renunciation is the cessation of this movement that allows consciousness to fall back into the heart-cave.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: renunciation (sannyāsa) as an inward movement, not mere outer abandonment; kin to Nivritti (Dim 16).
coordinate.key_relation: renunciation (sannyāsa) as an inward movement, not mere outer abandonment; kin to Nivritti (Dim 16)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When renunciation is understood as cessation of outward movement, one can be in the world but not move outward. The consciousness rests in the heart-cave.
facets.somatic: Body engaged in world but not dispersed in it, energy conserved, vitality maintained.
facets.emotional: Emotional steadiness without external dependence, contentment from within.
facets.cognitive: Mind without compulsive outward movement, naturally inward, centered.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces inwardly, releasing ownership and outcome while remaining fully engaged.

balanced.text:
When renunciation is understood as cessation of outward movement, one can be in the world but not move outward. The consciousness rests in the heart-cave.

Emotional: Emotional steadiness without external dependence, contentment from within.

Cognitive: Mind without compulsive outward movement, naturally inward, centered.

Psychic: The soul renounces inwardly, releasing ownership and outcome while remaining fully engaged.

excess.label: In excess

facets.somatic: Forced asceticism damaging body, neglect of legitimate physical needs.
facets.emotional: Emotional escape disguised as renunciation, suppressed desires creating tension, false saintliness.
facets.cognitive: Mind continues desiring while body is in monastery, dichotomy between outer and inner.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces only outwardly, dropping the robes and roles while the grasping continues within.

facets.emotional: Emotional life constantly outward, no inner anchor, dependence on external.
facets.cognitive: Mind always elsewhere, never present, scattered in desires.
facets.psychic: The soul renounces nothing, clutching every possession, role, and result as its own.

attributes.title: Heart Purification through Positive Accumulation
attributes.aspect: हृदय शुद्धि   ·   Chapter 22 The nature of this aspect. Heart purification is reversing the habit of negative accumulation. The same events that create misery can be approached positively—the person who was angry helped you study anger, helped you discover the witnessing capacity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: purifying the heart by accumulating the positive (virtue and devotion).
coordinate.key_relation: purifying the heart by accumulating the positive (virtue and devotion)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart accumulates positives—gratitude, beauty, witnessing. Negative experiences are seen as opportunities for awareness. The heart becomes pure, capable of meditation.
facets.somatic: Open chest, healthy heart, easy breathing, relaxed nervous system.
facets.emotional: Emotional positivity not as denial but as transformation, gratitude flowing naturally, peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind oriented toward learning from every experience, freedom from victim-thinking.
facets.psychic: The soul purifies its heart by steadily accumulating good — kindness, service, devotion — until the dross is crowded out.

facets.cognitive: Forced positive thinking that doesn't address real issues, denial mechanism.
facets.psychic: The soul keeps a proud ledger of its merits, the accounting feeding the ego it would purify.

attributes.title: Nyasa (Cosmic Placement Practice)
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 1, First Brahmana The nature of this aspect. Nyasa is the meditative practice of identifying parts of one's body with corresponding parts of the cosmic/divine body (e.g., dawn=head, sun=eyes, wind=breath, fire=mouth). Through systematic placement, the subject-object distinction collapses. This converts every ritual act into contemplation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: cosmic placement (nyāsa) — installing the divine in the body.
coordinate.key_relation: cosmic placement (nyāsa) — installing the divine in the body

facets.somatic: Body experienced as cosmos in miniature—each part with cosmic correspondence.
facets.emotional: Emotional unity with cosmic being through bodily identification.
facets.cognitive: Mind dissolves subject-object distinction through placement contemplation.
facets.psychic: The soul places the divine powers consciously throughout its body and life, consecrating the whole as sacred ground.

attributes.title: Smriti (Memory as Bridge)
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 7, Section 5 The nature of this aspect. Smriti is the faculty of retention that bridges time. Lower smriti chains us to past experiences and conditioning. Higher smriti (Brahma-smriti) maintains continuous remembrance of Brahman through all activities. The shift from lower to higher smriti is fundamental practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: memory (smṛti) as the bridge of continuity; kin to Smṛti (Dim 20).

facets.emotional: Emotional memory transformed toward devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mind continuously remembers higher reality.
facets.psychic: The soul uses memory as a bridge, carrying its learning and recollection of the Divine forward without being bound by the past.

attributes.title: Brahmacharya for Realization
attributes.aspect: ·   Chapter 8, Section 5 The nature of this aspect. Brahmacharya in its essential meaning is the channeling of all life-energy toward Brahman. Mere celibacy is a small part; the whole includes diet, association, speech, study, work, sleep—all directed toward the highest. This comprehensive discipline conserves and refines energy for realization.

facets.emotional: Emotional energy directed toward devotion.
facets.cognitive: Mental energy focused on highest pursuit.
facets.psychic: The soul conserves its vital energy for the sake of realization, the gathered force fueling the inner work.

attributes.title: Shatsampatti (Six-fold Foundation Wealth)
attributes.aspect: ·   Sadhana Chatushtaya The nature of this aspect. Shatsampatti is the spiritual seeker's six-fold wealth: 1) Sama - control of mind from objects; 2) Dama - control of senses; 3) Uparati - withdrawal from external practices when no longer needed; 4) Titiksha - patient endurance of heat-cold, pleasure-pain; 5) Samadhana - one-pointed concentration; 6) Shraddha - faith in teacher and scripture. Together, foundational treasure.

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.somatic: Body disciplined through dama and titiksha.
facets.emotional: Emotional stability through sama, samadhana.
facets.cognitive: Mental foundation of shraddha and viveka complete.
facets.psychic: The soul cultivates the six inner treasures — calm, control, withdrawal, endurance, faith, focus — as the wealth that steadies the path.

attributes.title: Yonimudra (Inner Vision Practice)
attributes.aspect: ·   Essence of the entire Gita The nature of this aspect. Yonimudra is a specific Kriya technique where the senses are sealed and inner vision opens. Combined with Pranayam, tongue-to-palate (kechari), bindu/sound listening, and star-meditation—these five form the operative essence of the entire Gita according to Lahiri. The technique is not philosophical but technical—body-engineering for spiritual experience.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: the inner-vision seal (yoni-mudrā); kin to the mudrās (Dim 26).
coordinate.key_relation: the inner-vision seal (yoni-mudrā); kin to the mudrās (Dim 26)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.somatic: Body's sensory channels sealed; inner perception activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional withdrawal from outer engagement, inner peace.
facets.cognitive: Mind sees inner reality directly.
facets.psychic: The soul turns the senses inward through the seal, withdrawing perception to behold the light within.

attributes.title: Sushumna Activation (Central Channel)
attributes.aspect: ·   Gandiva dhanu interpretation The nature of this aspect. Through Kriya practice, the Sushumna (central channel along the spine) becomes activated. Energy moves up from the base toward the head. Lahiri identifies Arjuna's bow Gandiva as the activated Sushumna. This is concrete energy work—not metaphor. The practitioner experiences energy moving in specific patterns.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: activating the central channel (suṣumnā); kin to the nāḍīs (Dim 6).

facets.cognitive: Mind clarified by sushumna activation.
facets.psychic: The soul awakens the central channel, energy rising along the spine to gather toward the higher centers.

attributes.title: Kechari Mudra (Tongue Practice)
attributes.aspect: ·   Five-step essence The nature of this aspect. Kechari mudra—the practice of folding the tongue back into the nasopharynx—is part of advanced Kriya. It is said to release amrita (nectar of immortality) and to facilitate breath-stillness and meditation. Lahiri counts it among the five essential techniques. Requires proper preparation and instruction.

facets.psychic: The soul, through the advanced seal, turns awareness inward and tastes the subtle nectar of absorption.

attributes.title: Pratibodha-Viditam (Self-Knowledge in Every Cognition)
attributes.aspect: प्रतिबोध-विदितम्   ·   Verse 2.4 The nature of this aspect. When Brahman is known as the inner Self in every state of consciousness, it is known truly. Not as a separate object to be known, but as the constant witness in every act of cognition.

facets.psychic: The soul recognizes the Self in every act of knowing, awareness known in and through each cognition.

attributes.attribute: Yoga as Steady Holding of Senses
attributes.title: Yoga as Steady Holding of Senses
attributes.aspect: स्थिर इन्द्रिय धारणा   ·   2.3.10-11 The nature of this aspect. When the five senses, mind, and intellect are still—this is the highest state, called yoga. Steady control of the senses is yoga. One must be careful, for yoga can be lost.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: yoga as the steady holding of the senses (Katha); kin to Pratyāhāra (Dim 11).

facets.emotional: Emotional stillness, depth.
facets.cognitive: Mind and intellect quietly held in yoga.
facets.psychic: The soul holds the senses steady, the firm but supple restraint that keeps the mind available for the inner work.

attributes.title: Guru's Test (Yama's Three-Day Silence)
attributes.aspect: गुरु परीक्षा   ·   1.1.7-9 The nature of this aspect. The genuine spiritual seeker is tested by the master's apparent indifference. Yama's three-day absence; the temptations he later offers Nachiketa (sons, wealth, kingdoms, celestial damsels)—all are tests. The disciple's patient waiting and refusal of substitutes reveal true readiness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the teacher’s test of readiness (Yama’s three-day silence, Katha).

facets.somatic: Body waits patiently, fasts willingly, endures discomfort.
facets.emotional: Emotional steadiness through perceived rejection, no offense at delays.
facets.cognitive: Mind refuses substitutes, holds clear vision of ultimate goal.
facets.psychic: The soul, like Naciketas waiting three days at Yama's door, proves its earnestness through patient, steady resolve.

attributes.title: Senses Outward, Self Inward (Parak-Pratyak)
attributes.aspect: पराक्-प्रत्यक्   ·   2.1.1 The nature of this aspect. By creation's design, senses go outward—everyone naturally looks externally. Only the rare heroic seeker (dhira), wanting immortality, turns the senses inward to behold the Pratyak-Atman (inward Self). This reversal of the natural current requires great courage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Framework — a vision / distinction · Valence: Neutral — a pointer to be realized · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Framework · Key relation: the senses turned outward vs the Self within (parāk / pratyāk, Katha); kin to the inward turn (Dim 28).

facets.somatic: Body's senses consciously turned inward at appropriate times.
facets.emotional: Emotional courage to face inner reality, not flee to external.
facets.cognitive: Mind heroic enough to turn against natural outward tendency.
facets.psychic: The soul reverses the outward flow, drawing the senses' attention back toward the inward Self.

attributes.aspect: तद्विज्ञानार्थम् स गुरुम्   ·   1.2.12 The nature of this aspect. For knowing that Truth, one must approach a guru with samit (fuel) in hand—a knower of Brahman, well-established in scriptures. Such approach requires humility, readiness to serve.

attributes.aspect: तपः-ब्रह्मचर्य-श्रद्धा   ·   3.1.5 The nature of this aspect. Through truth, austerity, right knowledge, brahmacharya (continence), and meditation is this Self perceived. By those who have purified themselves through inner discipline.

facets.overview: No spiritual discipline of any kind—life of indulgence, no preparation for Self-realization.
facets.somatic: Undisciplined body, depleted vitality.
facets.emotional: Emotional life without purification.
facets.cognitive: Mind without practice or preparation.
facets.psychic: The soul has none of the three, its practice without discipline, reserve, or conviction.

deficient.text:
No spiritual discipline of any kind—life of indulgence, no preparation for Self-realization.

Somatic: Undisciplined body, depleted vitality.

Emotional: Emotional life without purification.

Cognitive: Mind without practice or preparation.

Psychic: The soul has none of the three, its practice without discipline, reserve, or conviction.

attributes.title: Daharakasha (Small Space in the Heart)
attributes.aspect: दहराकाश   ·   8.1.1-8.4.3 The nature of this aspect. Within the city of Brahman (the body) is the small lotus of the heart. Within it is a small space (daharakasha). What is in this small space contains all this—heaven, earth, fire, wind, sun, moon, lightning, stars. All your desires, all your knowledge are there. It is unaffected by old age and death.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Vital · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: meditation on the small space in the heart (daharākāśa); same as Dahara Vidya (Dim 25).
coordinate.key_relation: meditation on the small space in the heart (daharākāśa); same as Dahara Vidya (Dim 25)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Heart-space meditation reveals the infinite within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner discovery.
facets.somatic: Body becomes vehicle to access inner space, heart center activated.
facets.emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, emotional contentment.
facets.cognitive: Mind enters the heart-space and discovers infinity within.
facets.psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the small cave of the heart, the whole cosmos found within.

balanced.text:
Heart-space meditation reveals the infinite within—the heart-cave contains all worlds. Genuine inner discovery.

Somatic: Body becomes vehicle to access inner space, heart center activated.

Emotional: Emotional discovery of vast inner space, emotional contentment.

Cognitive: Mind enters the heart-space and discovers infinity within.

Psychic: The soul discovers the vast space within the small cave of the heart, the whole cosmos found within.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery, mistaking imagination for realization.
facets.somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.
facets.emotional: Sentimental imagination without genuine discovery.
facets.cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.
facets.psychic: The soul fixates on heart-experiences and sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.

excess.text:
Forced visualization claiming heart-space discovery, mistaking imagination for realization.

Somatic: Body strained in forced visualization.

Emotional: Sentimental imagination without genuine discovery.

Cognitive: Concept-based visualization without realization.

Psychic: The soul fixates on heart-experiences and sensations, chasing the symbol over the awareness.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: No awareness of inner space—the heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.
facets.somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.
facets.emotional: Emotional heart unrecognized.
facets.cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.
facets.psychic: The soul never enters the heart-space, all its attention turned outward.

deficient.text:
No awareness of inner space—the heart-cave unknown, all attention turned outward.

Somatic: Body never settled enough to discover inner space.

Cognitive: Mind constantly outward, missing infinite within.

attributes.title: Shravana (Hearing the Truth)
attributes.aspect: ·   Three-stage learning The nature of this aspect. Shravana is the first stage of learning—hearing truth from the realized teacher. It is not academic listening but receptive presence to the teaching, especially the great pronouncements (Mahavakyas). The student must be qualified through Sadhana Chatushtaya before shravana bears fruit.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: hearing the truth (śravaṇa) — the first of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana.
coordinate.key_relation: hearing the truth (śravaṇa) — the first of śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Receptive, sustained hearing of truth from competent teacher. Whole being listens.
facets.somatic: Body still and present during teaching.
facets.emotional: Emotional openness to receive.
facets.cognitive: Mind quiet, receptive, attentive.
facets.psychic: The soul hears the liberating truth from a worthy source, the first and indispensable step of the path.

balanced.text:
Receptive, sustained hearing of truth from competent teacher. Whole being listens.

Somatic: Body still and present during teaching.

Emotional: Emotional openness to receive.

Cognitive: Mind quiet, receptive, attentive.

Psychic: The soul hears the liberating truth from a worthy source, the first and indispensable step of the path.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Endless hearing without contemplation or assimilation. Mere accumulation of teachings.
facets.somatic: Body always in classes without integration.
facets.emotional: Emotional dependence on teacher.
facets.cognitive: Mind hoarding teachings without digestion.
facets.psychic: The soul hears to accumulate doctrine, the listening feeding the intellect rather than the heart.

excess.text:
Endless hearing without contemplation or assimilation. Mere accumulation of teachings.

Somatic: Body always in classes without integration.

Emotional: Emotional dependence on teacher.

Cognitive: Mind hoarding teachings without digestion.

Psychic: The soul hears to accumulate doctrine, the listening feeding the intellect rather than the heart.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Distracted, partial hearing. Teachings don't penetrate. No foundation for further practice.
facets.somatic: Body present but attention elsewhere.
facets.emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind constructs arguments while pretending to listen.
facets.psychic: The soul never hears the truth at all, the teaching never reaching its ears.

deficient.text:
Distracted, partial hearing. Teachings don't penetrate. No foundation for further practice.

Somatic: Body present but attention elsewhere.

Emotional: No emotional opening to teaching.

attributes.title: Manana (Contemplation / Reflection)
attributes.aspect: ·   Three-stage learning The nature of this aspect. Manana is sustained reflection on what has been heard. It removes doubts through reasoning, examines apparent contradictions, integrates the teaching with experience. Without manana, shravana remains mere information. The teaching becomes one's own through this stage.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Practice — a contemplative / devotional discipline · Valence: Cultivated practice · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Means · Key relation: reflection on what is heard (manana) — the second of the three.
coordinate.key_relation: reflection on what is heard (manana) — the second of the three

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: Sustained reflection on teaching. Doubts addressed through careful reasoning. Teaching becomes one's own understanding.
facets.somatic: Body settled in contemplative posture.
facets.emotional: Emotional engagement with teaching.
facets.cognitive: Mind active in productive inquiry, doubt-removal.
facets.psychic: The soul reflects deeply on what it has heard, reasoning until doubt dissolves and the truth grows clear.

balanced.text:
Sustained reflection on teaching. Doubts addressed through careful reasoning. Teaching becomes one's own understanding.

Somatic: Body settled in contemplative posture.

Emotional: Emotional engagement with teaching.

Cognitive: Mind active in productive inquiry, doubt-removal.

Psychic: The soul reflects deeply on what it has heard, reasoning until doubt dissolves and the truth grows clear.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Over-analysis preventing assimilation. Endless intellectual mapping.
facets.somatic: Body in tension from over-analysis.
facets.emotional: Emotional dryness from over-intellectualization.
facets.cognitive: Mind generates more questions than it answers.
facets.psychic: The soul over-reasons endlessly, contemplation becoming a maze of analysis with no exit.

excess.text:
Over-analysis preventing assimilation. Endless intellectual mapping.

Somatic: Body in tension from over-analysis.

Emotional: Emotional dryness from over-intellectualization.

Cognitive: Mind generates more questions than it answers.

Psychic: The soul over-reasons endlessly, contemplation becoming a maze of analysis with no exit.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Heard teachings accepted without reflection. Doubts unaddressed; integration impossible.
facets.somatic: Body without contemplative engagement.
facets.emotional: Emotional resistance to deep examination.
facets.cognitive: Mind passive, no genuine inquiry.
facets.psychic: The soul never reflects on what it hears, the truth received but never made its own.

deficient.text:

attributes.title: Vairagya (Dispassion/Detachment) [Yoga Sutra Context]
attributes.aspect: वैराग्य   ·   Sutra 1.12, 1.15, 1.16 The nature of this aspect. Vairagya is dispassion—the consciousness of mastery over desire for seen and revealed objects. Patanjali distinguishes lower vairagya (mastery over visible objects) from higher (transcendence of all qualities of nature).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a cultivated quality · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Condition · Key relation: dispassion (vairāgya); with Abhyāsa, a wing of yoga; same as Vairāgya (Dim 23).
coordinate.key_relation: dispassion (vairāgya); with Abhyāsa, a wing of yoga; same as Vairāgya (Dim 23)

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Vairagya is established, the yogi can engage with the world without being controlled by desire. Lower vairagya frees from sensory objects; higher vairagya transcends the gunas themselves.
facets.somatic: Body free from compulsive sensory seeking, healthy engagement without bondage.
facets.emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-driven life, peace from non-grasping, equanimity.
facets.cognitive: Mental freedom from desires, clarity, mastery over the pull of objects, eventual transcendence of all attachment.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in dispassion, free from craving without suppression, holding the world's pleasures lightly.

balanced.text:
When Vairagya is established, the yogi can engage with the world without being controlled by desire. Lower vairagya frees from sensory objects; higher vairagya transcends the gunas themselves.

Somatic: Body free from compulsive sensory seeking, healthy engagement without bondage.

Emotional: Emotional freedom from desire-driven life, peace from non-grasping, equanimity.

Cognitive: Mental freedom from desires, clarity, mastery over the pull of objects, eventual transcendence of all attachment.

Psychic: The soul rests in dispassion, free from craving without suppression, holding the world's pleasures lightly.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Misunderstood Vairagya becomes aversion and rejection, harsh disengagement from life, cold withdrawal mistaken for dispassion.
facets.somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy engagement, neglect of body, ascetic damage.
facets.emotional: Emotional coldness disguised as detachment, false dispassion, rejection of warmth.
facets.cognitive: Mental rejection rather than transcendence, false superiority, escape from engagement.
facets.psychic: The soul forces a cold detachment, renunciation driven by aversion rather than understanding.

excess.text:
Misunderstood Vairagya becomes aversion and rejection, harsh disengagement from life, cold withdrawal mistaken for dispassion.

Somatic: Physical withdrawal from healthy engagement, neglect of body, ascetic damage.

Emotional: Emotional coldness disguised as detachment, false dispassion, rejection of warmth.

Cognitive: Mental rejection rather than transcendence, false superiority, escape from engagement.

Psychic: The soul forces a cold detachment, renunciation driven by aversion rather than understanding.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of Vairagya means being driven by every desire, no mastery over impulses, slavery to seen and revealed objects, no foundation for yoga.
facets.somatic: Body controlled by desires and impulses, no mastery, sensory slavery.
facets.emotional: Emotional bondage to desires, no peace from grasping, suffering from attachment.
facets.cognitive: Mental slavery to wanting, no freedom, perpetual seeking without satisfaction, no yogic foundation.
facets.psychic: The soul is ruled by craving, mistaking the next pleasure for the home it longs for.

deficient.text:
Absence of Vairagya means being driven by every desire, no mastery over impulses, slavery to seen and revealed objects, no foundation for yoga.

Somatic: Body controlled by desires and impulses, no mastery, sensory slavery.

Emotional: Emotional bondage to desires, no peace from grasping, suffering from attachment.

Cognitive: Mental slavery to wanting, no freedom, perpetual seeking without satisfaction, no yogic foundation.

Psychic: The soul is ruled by craving, mistaking the next pleasure for the home it longs for.

attributes.title: Kaivalya (Liberation/Aloneness)
attributes.aspect: कैवल्य   ·   Sutra 2.25, 3.55, 4.34 The nature of this aspect. Kaivalya is the ultimate goal of yoga—the absolute isolation/aloneness of pure consciousness from all that is not itself. The Self (Purusha) abides in its own true nature, freed from identification with prakriti.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic · Ontic mode: State / goal — liberation as aloneness (kaivalya) · Valence: The goal · Guṇa: Beyond the guṇas · Scale: Transcendent · Causal status: Fruit · Key relation: The final aim of the Yoga path (Dim 11); the Self resting in itself.
coordinate.key_relation: The final aim of the Yoga path (Dim 11); the Self resting in itself

balanced.label: Balanced / refined

facets.overview: When Kaivalya is achieved, consciousness rests in its own pure nature, free from all identification. This is the final liberation Patanjali points to.
facets.somatic: Body fully recognized as not-self, instrument used without identification, complete freedom from body-identification.
facets.emotional: Complete emotional liberation, freedom from all conditional happiness/unhappiness, abiding bliss.
facets.cognitive: Pure consciousness aware of itself, complete freedom from mental modifications, ultimate liberation, mind fully transcended.
facets.psychic: The soul rests in kaivalya, the aloneness of pure awareness abiding in its own nature, free of all entanglement.

balanced.text:
When Kaivalya is achieved, consciousness rests in its own pure nature, free from all identification. This is the final liberation Patanjali points to.

Somatic: Body fully recognized as not-self, instrument used without identification, complete freedom from body-identification.

Emotional: Complete emotional liberation, freedom from all conditional happiness/unhappiness, abiding bliss.

Cognitive: Pure consciousness aware of itself, complete freedom from mental modifications, ultimate liberation, mind fully transcended.

Psychic: The soul rests in kaivalya, the aloneness of pure awareness abiding in its own nature, free of all entanglement.

excess.label: In excess

facets.overview: Premature claims of Kaivalya without realization create spiritual delusion, dangerous self-deception, false transcendence claims.
facets.somatic: Body neglect from false transcendence claims, ungrounded existence.
facets.emotional: Emotional bypassing claiming liberation, false transcendence of legitimate human emotions.
facets.cognitive: Spiritual delusion claiming realization, dangerous self-deception, ego-claim of awakening.
facets.psychic: The soul grasps at kaivalya as a state to attain, the seeking itself an entanglement.

excess.text:
Premature claims of Kaivalya without realization create spiritual delusion, dangerous self-deception, false transcendence claims.

Somatic: Body neglect from false transcendence claims, ungrounded existence.

Emotional: Emotional bypassing claiming liberation, false transcendence of legitimate human emotions.

Cognitive: Spiritual delusion claiming realization, dangerous self-deception, ego-claim of awakening.

Psychic: The soul grasps at kaivalya as a state to attain, the seeking itself an entanglement.

deficient.label: Deficient

facets.overview: Absence of any aspiration toward Kaivalya means no orientation toward ultimate freedom, life lived in complete bondage without higher purpose.
facets.somatic: Body completely identified with as self, no transcendence orientation.
facets.emotional: Total emotional bondage, no aspiration for freedom, complete enslavement to conditions.
facets.cognitive: Mind completely identified with contents, no awareness of pure consciousness, no liberation orientation.
facets.psychic: The soul is wholly entangled in the play of nature, the standalone awareness never glimpsed.
